units.title: Server-side global bench cap: type, validation, enforcement, and 409 mapping
units.description: Lay the server foundation for the cap. Add the optional `benches.maxGlobal` field to `BenchSettings`, validate it in `PUT /api/settings` (positive integer or null), enforce it inside `createBench`'s synchronous reservation block, and map the new `GLOBAL_CAP_REACHED` `BenchError` to HTTP 409 in both create paths in `routes/benches.ts`. Fail open on settings.json corruption with one warning per process load. Demoable end-to-end via curl: `PUT /api/settings` with `benches: { maxGlobal: 2 }`, then create 2 benches, then watch the 3rd create return 409 with the new code. No UI yet; this slice ships the contract that every later UI slice will read.
acceptance_criteria: `BenchSettings` in `shared/types.ts` has an optional `maxGlobal?: number` field; `DEFAULT_BENCH_SETTINGS` is unchanged.
`PUT /api/settings` accepts `{ benches: { maxGlobal: 5 } }` (200) and rejects `0`, negative numbers, non-integers, NaN, Infinity, and non-number types with HTTP 400 naming `benches.maxGlobal`.
`PUT /api/settings` with `benches.maxGlobal: null` (or absent) persists with no cap.
`createBench` reads the current cap, rejects creation with a `BenchError('GLOBAL_CAP_REACHED', ...)` whenever `benches` Map size is already >= cap (in-flight `preparing` and `error` entries count).
`routes/benches.ts` maps that `BenchError` to HTTP 409 with the code `GLOBAL_CAP_REACHED` and a message naming current count and cap, in both the combined and plain create paths.
Two concurrent creates at cap-1 produce exactly one 201 and one 409.
`clearBench` is never blocked by the cap (the path used by `DELETE /benches/:id`, `autoClear`, and `workUnitAutoClear` is untouched).
Corrupt or unreadable settings.json causes the cap to be treated as unlimited and emits exactly one `console.warn` per process load.

units.title: Settings UI: rename Bench Defaults tab to Benches and add the Global bench limit control
units.description: Add the user-facing control for the cap. Rename `TAB_LABELS.benches` from 'Bench Defaults' to 'Benches' in `client/src/components/ProjectSettings.tsx`. Inside `BenchesTab`, add a new 'Global bench limit' section: a React Aria `RadioGroup` with `Unlimited` and `Limit` options paired with a numeric `TextField` that is disabled in Unlimited mode. Switching to Limit prefills 5. The control persists instantly like the rest of the Settings UI (no Save button): selecting a mode or blurring a valid value dispatches through `updateSettings` so the new `benches.maxGlobal` round-trips to `PUT /api/settings` per the contract WU-001 introduced. Surface inline validation for invalid numeric input.
acceptance_criteria: Top-level Settings tab is labelled 'Benches' (not 'Bench Defaults').
The Benches tab renders a 'Global bench limit' section above the existing content with a RadioGroup (Unlimited selected by default when no cap is set) and a numeric TextField.
Selecting Unlimited disables the TextField and immediately dispatches `benches.maxGlobal` as absent (cap removed).
Selecting Limit prefills 5; entering a valid positive integer persists `benches.maxGlobal` on blur and the value persists after reload.
Entering 0, -3, a non-integer, or leaving the field empty shows inline validation and is not persisted.
Changes persist immediately on selection/blur, consistent with the rest of the Settings UI (no separate Save button or confirmation toast).
The control follows the project's React Aria + Tailwind conventions; uses the same RadioGroup pattern as the existing Appearance section.

units.title: Disabled Set up bench button with accessible tooltip when at cap
units.description: Make the cap visible at the point of creation. Add a new `useGlobalCap` derived hook (`client/src/hooks/useGlobalCap.ts`) that composes `useSettings()` and `useAllBenches()` into a `GlobalCapState` ({ isCapped, isAtCap, isOverCap, current, max }). Wrap the 'Set up bench' Button in `BenchesTab.tsx` with a `TooltipTrigger`; set `isDisabled` from `useGlobalCap.isAtCap || isOverCap`; render the tooltip text 'Global bench limit reached. N of M benches in use. Clear a bench to free a slot.' Apply the same disabled state to the confirm Button in `CreateBenchModal.tsx` so a stale client still surfaces the server 409 via the existing `setError` flow as defense in depth.
acceptance_criteria: New file `client/src/hooks/useGlobalCap.ts` exports a hook returning `{ isCapped, isAtCap, isOverCap, current, max }`.
`BenchesTab.tsx` wraps the 'Set up bench' Button in `TooltipTrigger`; `isDisabled` is true exactly when at or over cap.
Tooltip text is exactly: 'Global bench limit reached. N of M benches in use. Clear a bench to free a slot.', associated via `aria-describedby`.
The button uses React Aria's `Button` `isDisabled` (not native `disabled`), so it remains focusable.
`CreateBenchModal.tsx` confirm Button disables under the same condition, and the existing `setError` flow renders the 409 message if a stale client somehow submits.
When the cap is unset or current < cap, the button behaves exactly as before.

units.title: GlobalBenchMeter on the Benches tab header
units.description: Add the always-on awareness affordance. Create `client/src/components/GlobalBenchMeter.tsx` mirroring the `ProjectTile.tsx` meter markup. Render it inside `BenchesTab.tsx`'s header strip next to the 'Set up bench' button, but only when `useGlobalCap().isCapped` is true. Implement the staged colour ramp: neutral (`stone-400`) below 80% of cap, `amber-500` at >= 80%, `red-500` at >= 100% (including the over-cap state after a user lowers the cap below the current count). Expose `aria-label` of exactly the form 'Global benches: N of M'.
acceptance_criteria: New file `client/src/components/GlobalBenchMeter.tsx` renders the global meter using the existing per-Project tile meter pattern.
The meter is rendered in `BenchesTab.tsx`'s header only when a cap is set; it is omitted when the cap is null.
Visible count text shows `N / M` (or equivalent) using JetBrains Mono per project typography.
Colour ramp: stone-400 below 80%, amber-500 at >= 80%, red-500 at >= 100%; fill width clamps at 100% when current > max.
`aria-label` is exactly `Global benches: <current> of <max>`.
Reuses `useGlobalCap` from WU-003; no duplicated count derivation.
